paraphrase: Tier3 adds handling, shared Poisson arrivals, frequency noise and faults on top of Tier2 physics; parameters are assumptions, not a field digital twin.
The 0714 S3 task explicitly excluded real arrival queues, real-time KPI calculation and multi-act fault storytelling.
Three waiting boxes, one transported cargo mesh and the queue chart are independently created and have no shared good identity.
The earlier approved design called for physical queue boxes and one event schema driving 3D and KPI snapshots.
The current Tier3 simulator is a single-server mixed-flow event model with shared arrivals and equipment downtime, but it returns aggregate metrics rather than a visualization trace.
The formal animation has a hand-built multi-good event list; it states that sim export should replace it, and the slot-fault reroute is staged.
Canonical U2 assigns laden vertical factor 0.80 and 6/8.5/12.5 second handling tiers to Tier3.
realism.py still uses a constant 15-second handling value and does not call the U2 laden travel function.
A runtime camera can move and a 3D window can follow it to keep moving objects under observation.
A collector listens to 3D/process events and writes event values to rows, supporting one-event-source data synchronization.
Queue lengths, server utilization and entity movements can be visualized from the same simulation behavior.
HMI design should reduce operator error and improve situational awareness.
Lack of interpretability can block adoption because users may not understand or trust optimization recommendations.
